revisionRequest.description: Replace inline SVG in card-testimonial.php with theme_block_asset() call
revisionRequest.rationale: Project convention requires all SVG assets to be loaded via theme_block_asset() instead of being inlined. The inline chevron arrow SVG in parts/card-testimonial.php (lines 61-63) violated this convention.

implementation.svgContent: Extracted the inline SVG path data and created ChevronRight.svg file with viewBox='0 0 24 24', preserving the chevron path: d='M9 6L15 12L9 18' with stroke properties
implementation.sharedPartStrategy: Since parts/card-testimonial.php is a shared template part used by both TestimonialsCarousel and Archive blocks, and theme_block_asset() relies on the global $takt_current_block_folder set during block rendering, the SVG file was created in BOTH blocks' resources directories. This ensures the asset loads correctly regardless of which block is rendering the card.
implementation.cssClasses: Wrapped the theme_block_asset() call in a <span> element with the same classes that were on the inline SVG (w-5 h-4 transition-transform group-hover:translate-x-1). This preserves the size and hover animation behavior.
implementation.ariaHidden: The aria-hidden='true' attribute is already in the SVG file itself, so it's preserved when the asset is loaded.

themeBlockAssetFunction.behavior: theme_block_asset() uses the global $takt_current_block_folder variable to determine which block is currently being rendered. It loads assets from public/blocks/{BlockName}/{file}. The function is called during block render_callback execution (lines 18-47 in blocks.php), where $takt_current_block_folder is set to the current block's absolute path.

sharedPartChallenge.problem: parts/card-testimonial.php is used by multiple blocks (TestimonialsCarousel via get_template_part() and Archive via a card map). When theme_block_asset() is called from within the part, it uses the current block context.
sharedPartChallenge.solution: Create the ChevronRight.svg file in BOTH blocks' resources directories. When TestimonialsCarousel renders the card, theme_block_asset() loads from blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/resources/. When Archive renders the card, it loads from blocks/Archive/resources/. This is the simplest and most maintainable approach.
alternativesConsidered: Shared assets directory: Would require a different helper function or modifying theme_block_asset() to support shared paths, which violates the existing architecture.
Conditional logic in the part: Would require detecting which block is rendering and loading different assets, adding unnecessary complexity.
Inline SVG only for shared parts: Would create an exception to the convention, making the codebase inconsistent.

buildProcess.copyMechanism: The build process (wp-scripts) automatically copies all files from blocks/{BlockName}/resources/ to public/blocks/{BlockName}/ during the build. The ChevronRight.svg files were successfully copied to the public directory.
buildProcess.verification: Confirmed files exist at public/blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/ChevronRight.svg (217 bytes) and public/blocks/Archive/ChevronRight.svg (217 bytes)

before.code:
<svg class="w-5 h-4 transition-transform group-hover:translate-x-1" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true">
	<path d="M9 6L15 12L9 18" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
</svg>
before.issues: Inline SVG violates project convention, makes the template harder to read, duplicates SVG markup in the HTML output for every card

after.benefits: Follows project convention, cleaner template code, SVG is loaded from asset file and can be cached by browser, easier to update the icon in the future (one file instead of finding all inline instances)

verificationSteps.action: Created ChevronRight.svg in both blocks' resources directories
verificationSteps.result: SUCCESS - Files created with correct SVG markup
SUCCESS - Inline SVG replaced with asset call, wrapped in span to preserve CSS classes
SUCCESS - Build passed with only size warnings (no new errors)

verificationSteps.action: Verified SVG files copied to public directory
verificationSteps.result: SUCCESS - Both files exist in public/blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/ and public/blocks/Archive/

notes: The ChevronRight.svg file is identical in both blocks' resources directories—this is intentional and acceptable duplication for the shared part pattern

The <span> wrapper is necessary to apply the CSS classes (w-5 h-4 transition-transform group-hover:translate-x-1) that were previously on the <svg> element. The theme_block_asset() function only outputs the SVG content, not a wrapper element.
The aria-hidden='true' attribute is included in the SVG file itself, so it's preserved when loaded
This change affects both TestimonialsCarousel and Archive blocks since they both use the card-testimonial part
The fix follows the exact same pattern used for other SVG assets in the theme (ArrowLeft.svg, ArrowRight.svg, Pause.svg, etc.)
